commit.message:
Fix burndown projection consistency across color modes

Use aggregate rate with proportional distribution instead of
independent per-group rates, so the projected total is identical
regardless of color grouping.

Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)
via [Happy](https://happy.engineering)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-Authored-By: Happy <yesreply@happy.engineering>

feat(uv): Support [tool.uv] no-binary-package (#831)

## Summary

- Reads `[tool.uv] no-binary-package` from `pyproject.toml` to identify
packages that must be built from source
- For listed packages: forces sdist build (even for -none-any wheels),
excludes all pre-built wheels from the install config
- Adds a safety `fail()` if a no-binary package has no sdist in the
lockfile

This mirrors uv's own `no-binary-package` behavior, enabling use cases
like building C extensions with custom/debug interpreters.

## Test plan

- [x] All 44 main tests pass (`bazel test //...`)
- [x] All 16 e2e tests pass (`cd e2e && bazel test //...`)
- [ ] Stacked PR #830 exercises this with source-built debug Python +
markupsafe sdist

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: claude <claude@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add streaming transcription and login item support

Replace batch transcription with a streaming loop that transcribes
audio in 3-second chunks while recording, reducing perceived latency.
Add a "Start at Login" menu item that installs/removes a LaunchAgent
plist to auto-start the app on login.

- Lock audio_data access with a threading.Lock
- Save and restore clipboard once per recording session instead of
  per paste, avoiding clipboard thrash
- Remove wave/tempfile dependency; transcribe numpy arrays directly
- Pass vad_filter=True to reduce hallucinations on silence

commit.message:
feat(reocr): persist OCR result as .ocr file in work folder

When re-OCR completes, write result to data/{slug}/{page}.ocr alongside
the image. On editor mount, check for .ocr file first (survives restarts),
fall back to localStorage job_id for in-progress polling. Apply deletes
the file; dismiss keeps it for later; explicit 'Kustuta OCR fail' button
also deletes. Backend: GET/DELETE /admin/work/{id}/page-ocr endpoints.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: patch psycopg_pool at import + strip top_p for Anthropic API

Two Mem0 compatibility fixes:

1. Subclass psycopg_pool.ConnectionPool before Mem0 import to inject
   prepare_threshold=0 into ALL pools Mem0 creates — including the one
   used during __init__ (list_cols). Previous approaches (config param,
   post-init replace) were too late.

2. Monkey-patch LLM._get_common_params to remove top_p — Anthropic API
   rejects requests with both temperature and top_p set, but Mem0's
   AnthropicConfig defaults both to 0.1.

arch: separate protocol and marketplace into independent layers

- cmd/node: pure protocol binary (identity, credit, settlement, verification, reputation, economics)
- cmd/marketplace: application binary that connects to protocol via SDK
- Protocol API serves only primitive endpoints
- Marketplace proxies protocol endpoints + adds task/escrow/routing/explorer
- docker-compose.yml: two-service deployment (protocol + marketplace)
- Dockerfile: combined binary with --marketplace flag for backward compatibility
- Marketplace uses same SDK as any third-party builder
- Third parties can deploy protocol node without marketplace code
- No behavior changes — all existing functionality preserved

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Source Documents field to accumulate multiple files per contact

The /forms/upload-custom-files endpoint replaces the entire field value
on each call. To preserve existing files, we now:
1. Fetch the contact and read existing files in the Source Documents field
2. Re-download each existing file's bytes via its GHL download URL
3. POST all existing files + the new file together in a single request

This ensures every image for a lead accumulates rather than overwriting.

Co-Authored-By: Claude (claude-opus-4-6) <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(client): add score progression graph (#109)

* feat(client): add score progression graph with round-by-round team scores

Track cumulative team scores after each round on the server (GameInstance)
and send the history to clients via game:round-end, game:ended, and
reconnect:success events. A custom SVG line chart shows both teams'
scores over time with a winning score threshold line.

- ScoreProgressionChart: responsive SVG with auto-scaled axes, gridlines,
  team-colored polylines, and data point circles
- ScoreChartModal: accessible from a chart icon button in the top bar
- GameEndModal: embeds the chart between final scores and "Play Again"
- Full reconnect support: score history restored on rejoin

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix calendar ICS feed emitting UTC times instead of local timezone (#61)

ical-generator reads Date components via local getters (getHours, etc.)
which return UTC values on Node.js servers. When stamped with TZID, this
made events appear at UTC time (e.g., 11pm) instead of local time (6pm
Central). Fix by converting UTC dates to wall-clock representation in the
trip's preferred timezone before passing to ical-generator.

commit.message:
fix(appro): fix dashboard 500 error + add password visibility toggle

Root cause: lastPhysicalStock column had NULL values in DB but was
declared non-nullable in Prisma schema. Migration 20250128 added the
column without NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, leaving existing rows with NULL.

Fixes:
- Add migration to set NULL → 0, add NOT NULL DEFAULT, cast to DECIMAL(15,4)
- Apply to both products_mp and products_pf tables
- Revert diagnostic try-catch in appro controller (no longer needed)
- Add show/hide password toggle on login form to help verify credentials
- Add autoComplete attributes to email and password inputs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add spawn status command to show live server state (#2254)

Implements the `spawn status` command requested in #2253. The command:
- Reads active (non-deleted) cloud servers from history
- Queries Hetzner and DigitalOcean REST APIs in parallel using saved tokens
- Shows a live-state table: ID, Agent, Cloud, IP, State, Since
- States: running (green), stopped (yellow), gone (dim), unknown (dim)
- --prune flag marks gone servers as deleted in history
- --json flag outputs machine-readable JSON for scripting
- `spawn ps` is an alias for `spawn status`

Other clouds (AWS, GCP, Sprite, Daytona) require CLI auth flows that cannot
run non-interactively; they report "unknown" with a helpful hint.

Agent: issue-fixer

Co-authored-by: B <6723574+louisgv@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(build): add vercel.json to resolve vite command not found

Vercel auto-detected Vite and ran `vite build` directly, bypassing
node_modules/.bin. Explicitly set buildCommand to `npm run build`.

Fix TypeScript build error: replace TrackedPlaylistDTO intersection with explicit PlaylistDetailResponse type

TrackedPlaylistDTO has tracks?: TrackedTrackDTO[] which caused TypeScript
to resolve the intersection as TrackedTrackDTO[], failing TrackRow's
TrackWithHistory prop check.

commit.message:
[fix] audit: thread safety, dead code, cross-platform warnings, suite links

- llm_stream.hpp: replace #include <cstring> (unused) with <mutex>
- llm_stream.hpp: add curl_global_init_once() via std::call_once so
  concurrent stream() calls don't race on libcurl global state
- llm_stream.hpp: remove dead StreamCtx::done field; the flag was set
  but never read after curl_easy_perform returns
- CMakeLists.txt: use generator expressions for -Wall/-Wextra so the
  build doesn't emit unknown-option warnings under MSVC (/W4 instead)
- examples/chat_loop.cpp: document the history serialization limitation
- README.md: add "See Also" table linking the full header suite

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix RecurringPayment classification using jobRecurringId from order items

Keap does not populate subscription_plan at the order level for rebills.
Instead, recurring subscription charges have jobRecurringId > 0 on their
order items. Replace the broken subscription_plan check with a jobRecurringId
scan — if any item has jobRecurringId > 0, classify as RecurringPayment.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Improve PDF text-to-HTML formatting with pre-processing step

Adds a preProcess() pass before block splitting to fix common PDF
extraction artifacts: strips page numbers stuck to text (e.g. "4Labor"
→ "Labor"), inserts paragraph breaks around bullet runs so inline
bullets get proper <ul> rendering, and adds breaks before heading-like
lines that were getting merged into body paragraphs.

commit.message:
fix: electrical onboarding bugs (rounds 2-3 QA findings)

- BUG #506: FAQ quick-add now pulls actual tenant hours instead of
  hardcoded 'Saturday 10AM-2PM'. Added buildFullHoursString() that
  groups days by their hours (handles weekday vs weekend differences).
  Service/general FAQ templates now use [hours] placeholder.

- BUG #504: Guided Setup Step 1 no longer shows 'Complete' with only
  business name filled. Added local address check in GuidedSetupFlow
  so business-info step requires address to be non-empty.

- BUG #505: Replaced .single() with .maybeSingle() in useBusinessCapabilities
  (ai_assistants, booking_delivery_settings, dispatch_delivery_settings) and
  useBrainSummaries (tenants, ai_assistants, dispatch_delivery_settings).
  .single() returns HTTP 406 when 0 rows found — causes console errors on
  every Brain page load for new tenants.

- BUG #503/#508: ServiceCatalogEditor empty state now shows a
  'Load N electrical services' button when tenant has an industry slug
  with catalog entries. Seeds all industry services in one click.

commit.message:
feat: Add WhatsApp notifications via CallMeBot

- notifications/whatsapp.py: send_alert() + format_agent_alert()
- Only sends between 08:00-22:00 Asia/Jerusalem time
- URL-encodes message, 10s timeout, silent on HTTP errors
- base_agent._notify_failure() now formats the Hebrew alert message
- 25 tests covering time window, credentials, URL construction, errors

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

[#2186] Phase 2 Core + Phase 3: Engine & Tracker (#2195, #2196, #2197, #2202, #2203) (#2232)

* [#2195] Add symphony worker process skeleton

- New src/symphony-worker/ directory with polling event loop
- channels.ts: LISTEN/NOTIFY channel definitions
- config.ts: per-project config loading from symphony_orchestrator_config
- heartbeat.ts: orchestrator heartbeat with HeartbeatManager
- recovery.ts: startup recovery sweep for orphaned runs
- health.ts: health server on configurable port (SYMPHONY_HEALTH_PORT)
- metrics.ts: Prometheus metrics for symphony worker
- run.ts: main entry point with DB pool, listener, graceful shutdown
- E5: document multi-orchestrator LISTEN/NOTIFY behavior
- P2-1: configurable health port, fail fast on EADDRINUSE
- 39 unit tests across 6 test files

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [#2203] Add GitHub rate limit management

- rate-limit.ts: rate limit tracking infrastructure
- parseRateLimitHeaders(): parse X-RateLimit-* from GitHub responses
- checkRateLimit(): query current rate limit status per resource
- reserveBudget(): reserve API call budget with minimum reserve
- recordApiCall(): upsert rate limit data from response headers
- calculatePollingInterval(): distribute polling across time window
- Prometheus metrics: symphony_github_rate_remaining, symphony_github_api_calls_total
- P3-3: clear API surface for consumers (#2202 etc.)
- 22 unit tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [#2196] Implement orchestrator state machine (22 states)

- 22-state RunState enum with PascalCase keys → snake_case DB values (X1 finding)
- Compare-and-swap transitions via state_version (CAS)
- claim_epoch fencing for split-brain prevention
- Terminal state idempotency (double-finalization is no-op)
- Per-state timeout definitions for watchdog sweep
- 11 failure classes with per-class retry limits and recovery strategies
- Advisory stage inference from terminal I/O (never drives transitions)
- Transition event recording to symphony_run_event
- DB migration 148: updates symphony_run CHECK constraint to 22-state lifecycle
- 77 unit tests covering states, transitions, CAS, fencing, timeouts, failure taxonomy

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [#2197] Implement claim & concurrency control

- Atomic claim acquisition with FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED
- pg_advisory_xact_lock for serialized concurrency checks
- Four-level concurrency enforcement in single transaction:
  1. Global: max_concurrent_agents - running_total
  2. Project: project.max_concurrent - running_in_project
  3. Host: host.max_concurrent_sessions - active_on_host
  4. State: per_state_limit[state] - running_in_state
- Deterministic advisory lock ordering: global → project → host → state
- claim_epoch monotonic counter for fencing
- Claim lease timeout (60s) with renewal and stale expiration
- Idempotency key support for safe claim retries
- Candidate selection: priority ASC, created_at ASC, identifier tiebreaker
- 19 unit tests covering all concurrency levels and edge cases

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [#2202] Implement GitHub Issue Sync — pluggable tracker, GitHub adapter, sync service

- Pluggable tracker interface with abstract types (Tracker, NormalizedIssue, TrackerPage)
- GitHub adapter with paginated/resumable sync, rate-limit-aware via RateLimitBudget
- Sync hash computation for drift detection (SHA-256 of normalized fields)
- SyncService: hierarchy creation (initiative + epic), issue sync, reconciliation
- Migration 148: github_issue_sync table, project_repository extensions
- Mock rate limit budget for use until #2203 delivers real implementation
- 52 unit tests (normalization, sync hash, pagination, rate limit)
- 19 integration tests (migration schema, hierarchy, sync, reconciliation)

* [#2196] Fix Codex review findings (6 issues)

1. High: Make Succeeded reachable — add IssueClosing → Succeeded transition
2. High: Fix claim_epoch NULL bypass — reject when caller provides epoch
   but run has no epoch (split-brain protection)
3. High: Fix idempotency key — store and match on actual key in DB, add
   idempotency_key column to symphony_claim
4. Medium: Fix advisory lock overlap — use two-parameter
   pg_advisory_xact_lock(namespace, key) to prevent false contention
5. Medium: Fix migration data transform — convert old status values before
   adding new CHECK constraint
6. Tests: Add reachability test, NULL epoch test, idempotency key tests,
   100 tests now passing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [#2202] Address Codex review findings — SSRF, races, atomicity, logging

- SSRF: Validate apiBaseUrl (HTTPS only, allowed GitHub hosts)
- Race condition: Catch unique violation on concurrent create, retry as update
- Atomicity: Wrap updateSyncedIssue in transaction
- Monotonic: Use GREATEST() for last_synced_at updates
- Logging: Add console.warn for swallowed errors in reconciliation/fetch
- Tests: 9 new SSRF validation tests (61 unit total)

* [#2186] Renumber GitHub issue sync migration from 148 to 149

* [#2186] Align worker and sync-service with 22-state model from migration 148

Worker run.ts, recovery.ts, and sync-service.ts used legacy status names
(queued, claiming, timed_out, executing) that no longer exist after
migration 148 introduces the 22-state lifecycle.

- Import RunState enum and TERMINAL_STATES from symphony/states.ts
- Worker: queued -> unclaimed, claiming -> claimed
- Recovery: use new state transitions (claimed->failed, running->stalled, paused->orphaned)
- Sync service: parameterized terminal state queries
- Updated recovery tests to match new state names

* [#2186] Fix integration tests for 22-state model and new migration numbering

- symphony_schema_migration.test.ts: update INSERT values to use new
  status names (unclaimed, running, stalled, etc.) and new stage names
  (reading_issue, planning, coding, etc.) from migration 148
- dev-session-schema-migration.test.ts: adjust rollback step counts
  to account for two new migrations (148, 149) added after 147

---------

Co-authored-by: security-agent-1 <security-agent-1@devcontainer-security.local>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
[refactor] Remove LLM paper critic — peer review is the quality gate

The LLM paper_critic phase was redundant with peer review and caused
most revision loop bugs (49K char responses, scope creep, internal
section targeting, infinite retry cycles).

Replaced with: programmatic quality gate only (word counts, citation
counts, structural completeness) → output assembly → peer review.

Removed:
- _pipeline_paper_critic method (110 lines)
- paper_critic from model routing
- The 3-cycle LLM evaluation + revision loop

Kept:
- _pre_critic_validation (programmatic checks + auto-rewrite)
- _check_section_quality (per-section mechanical checks)
- "paper_critic" checkpoint name (for resume compatibility)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
